sections.title: Overview
sections.content: This guideline provides practical injury-prevention advice for remote-site sport and gym activity.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Recreational training on site
- Recurrent strains, sprains, or overload symptoms
- Team sport or individual exercise participation

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Prevention should be simple enough to be used consistently
- Warm-up, gradual load progression, recovery, and appropriate technique usually matter more than elaborate corrective programs
- Sleep, fatigue, hydration, and alcohol use can materially increase injury risk in camp environments

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Identify whether the participant is returning after injury, illness, or prolonged layoff
- Encourage warm-up, progressive loading, and avoidance of sharp unplanned volume spikes
- Escalate for persistent pain or recurrent instability rather than repeatedly self-managing the same injury

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Use role-specific advice for running, lifting, change-of-direction sports, and mixed-mode training
- Address footwear, surface, fatigue, and prior injury history in risk reduction planning
- Encourage early review when small recurring symptoms begin to alter mechanics or confidence

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Prevention programs work best when they are short, repeatable, and matched to the actual activity people do
